facts: 7,848 training (non-representative; spec-confirmed DDF) + 3.5M test (fair LSST survey); 14 classes + class-99 unknown.
hypotheses: Class-99 (unknown-class) detection is categorically distinct from covariate shift.
The deliberate non-representativeness produces measurable shift rather than methods merely learning class proxies.
intuitions: 2024-25 photometric classification post-LSST-first-light; ZTF live-test comparisons.
unknowns: Class-99 is monolithic — no within-novel-class structure.
The spectroscopic-vs-photometric label-modality difference cannot be cleanly classified as OOD versus label-noise.
